name: politiclaw_configure
rationale: Best first-run and reconfiguration entry point because it folds address setup, rep bootstrap, issue-setup handoff, monitoring cadence, and API key persistence into one user-facing flow.
label: Configure PolitiClaw

name: politiclaw_issue_stances
rationale: Single tool for setting, listing, and deleting declared issue stances. Directly expresses user priorities and powers bill and rep alignment.
label: Manage declared issue stances (set, list, delete)

name: politiclaw_record_stance_signal
rationale: Low-level preference telemetry for flows and monitoring, not a normal direct user action.
label: Record PolitiClaw stance signal

name: politiclaw_get_my_reps
rationale: Directly answers a common question and is foundational to later rep-scoring workflows.
label: Get my federal representatives

name: politiclaw_score_representative
rationale: One of the clearest user-value tools: how a rep's federal roll-call votes (House and Senate) line up with declared issues.
label: Did this representative represent the stances you declared?

name: politiclaw_rep_report
rationale: Batch version of rep scoring, useful for digests and power users more than casual queries.
label: Did your delegation represent the stances you declared?

name: politiclaw_search_bills
rationale: Natural first step for bill exploration and a good front door to legislative tracking.
label: Search recent federal bills

name: politiclaw_get_bill_details
rationale: Detailed inspection tool that is valuable after a bill has already been identified.
label: Fetch a single federal bill

name: politiclaw_score_bill
rationale: Turns raw bill lookup into a user-relevant answer by mapping it to declared issues.
label: Score a bill against your declared stances

name: politiclaw_ingest_votes
rationale: Data-ingestion plumbing that supports rep scoring but is not a user-facing civic task.
label: Ingest recent congressional roll-call votes

name: politiclaw_get_my_ballot
rationale: Raw ballot snapshot is useful as plumbing, but politiclaw_election_brief is the better public entry point.
label: Preview ballot logistics and contests for your saved address

name: politiclaw_election_brief
rationale: Best ballot front door because it bundles setup checks, contest framing, and rep context in one readable guide.
label: One readable election guide: ballot framing + rep alignment + setup checks

name: politiclaw_check_upcoming_votes
rationale: Great for engaged monitoring, but more procedural than the core user journeys.
label: Check upcoming votes + bill changes since last run

name: politiclaw_mutes
rationale: Single tool for adding, removing, and listing monitoring mutes — useful tuning control once monitoring is already in use.
label: Manage monitoring alert mutes (add, remove, list)

name: politiclaw_create_reminder
rationale: Proactive bookmark tool used alongside monitoring; helpful for power users tracking a specific vote or election.
label: Create a reminder anchored to a bill, event, or election

name: politiclaw_action_moments
rationale: Single tool for listing open offer packages and dismissing them with per-offer feedback (useful / not_now / stop) so users can tune action suggestions without muting entire targets.
label: List or dismiss open action-package offers

name: politiclaw_research_finance
rationale: Directly maps to common election questions: per-candidate FEC finance lookup or side-by-side challenger comparison for stored reps.
label: Research candidate finance — single candidate or side-by-side challengers

name: politiclaw_draft_outreach
rationale: Clear outcome-oriented action that follows naturally from bills, reps, and issue stances. Single tool covering both letter and call-script formats.
label: Draft a letter or call script for a representative

name: politiclaw_doctor
rationale: Best recovery entry point when anything about setup, data, or monitoring looks broken.
label: Diagnose PolitiClaw install health
